  "text": "## Privilege and RBAC Enforcement in TiDB\n\n### PrivilegeManager Interface\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privilege.go`\nDefines the `Manager` interface with methods: `RequestVerification`, `RequestVerificationWithUser`, `RequestDynamicVerification`, `RequestDynamicVerificationWithUser`, `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege`, `ConnectionVerification`, `ShowGrants`, `ActiveRoles`, `FindEdge`, `GetDefaultRoles`, `GetAllRoles`, `DBIsVisible`, `IsDynamicPrivilege`, `MatchIdentity`, and others. Also defines `BindPrivilegeManager`/`GetPrivilegeManager` to attach/retrieve the Manager from a session context. `VerificationInfo` struct holds connection verification results.\n\nFile: `pkg/privilege/conn/conn.go`\nDefines the `AuthConn` interface used during connection authentication for LDAP/plugin auth.\n\n### MySQLPrivilege Cache\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\nThis is the central privilege cache. Key types:\n- `MySQLPrivilege` (line 287): in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables (user, db, tables_priv, columns_priv, global_priv, global_grants, role_edges, default_roles). Contains both `immutable` (raw slices) and `extended` (accelerated maps: UserMap, DBMap, TablesPrivMap, Dynamic, Global).\n- `Handle` (line 1818): thread-safe wrapper around `MySQLPrivilege` using `atomic.Pointer`. `Handle.Get()` returns the current snapshot; `Handle.Update()` reloads from KV; `ensureActiveUser()` lazily loads per-user data.\n- Record types: `UserRecord`, `dbRecord`, `tablesPrivRecord`, `columnsPrivRecord`, `globalPrivRecord`, `dynamicPrivRecord`, `defaultRoleRecord`, `roleGraphEdgesTable`.\n- SQL queries: `sqlLoadRoleGraph`, `sqlLoadUserTable`, `sqlLoadDBTable`, `sqlLoadTablePrivTable`, `sqlLoadColumnsPrivTable`, `sqlLoadGlobalPrivTable`, `sqlLoadGlobalGrantsTable`, `sqlLoadDefaultRoles`.\n- `LoadAll()` loads all tables; `LoadRoleGraph()` loads `mysql.role_edges`; individual `Load*Table()` methods load specific tables.\n\n### RequestVerification and Callers\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go`\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestVerification()` (line 142): Top-level static privilege check for current user. Handles SEM (Security Enhanced Mode) restrictions, memory/system DB rules, extension access check functions, then delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification()`.\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestVerificationWithUser()` (line 205): Same but for an explicitly specified user identity.\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestDynamicVerification()` (line 126): Checks dynamic (named string) privileges, falls back to SUPER if SEM not enabled.\n- `UserPrivileges.HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege()` (line 113): Checks dynamic priv WITHOUT SUPER fallback.\n\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\n- `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification()` (line 1333): Core multi-level check. Resolves all effective roles via `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles`, then checks global user priv → db priv → table priv → column priv in order.\n- `MySQLPrivilege.RequestDynamicVerification()` (line 1311): Checks dynamic privileges; falls back to SUPER if not restricted.\n- `MySQLPrivilege.HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege()` (line 1287): Iterates dynamic privilege records for all effective roles.\n\nCallers in `pkg/planner/`:\n- `pkg/planner/optimize.go` (line 501-505): After building the logical plan, calls `core.VisitInfo4PrivCheck()` then `core.CheckPrivilege(activeRoles, pm, visitInfo)` to enforce privileges.\n- `pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go`, `CheckPrivilege()` (line 126): Iterates `visitInfo` entries; for each static priv calls `pm.RequestVerification()`; for dynamic privs calls `pm.RequestDynamicVerification()`.\n- `pkg/planner/core/preprocess.go` (line 513): During preprocessing, calls `pm.RequestVerification()` directly for table access denial when the table is not found.\n- `pkg/planner/core/point_get_plan.go`, `checkFastPlanPrivilege()` (line 1502): Calls `pm.RequestVerification()` for point-get fast path plans.\n- `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (lines 5009, 5016): Calls `pm.RequestVerification()` for view SELECT checks and `pm.RequestVerificationWithUser()` for view definer checks.\n- `pkg/planner/core/plan_cache.go`, `checkPreparedPriv()` (line 328): Calls `core.CheckPrivilege()` for prepared statement execution.\n- `pkg/planner/core/expression_codec_fn.go` (line 91): Calls `checker.RequestVerification()` for codec function table access checks.\n\n### Role Graph Resolution\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\n- `roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 249): Maps role key → `*auth.RoleIdentity`; represents edges in the role graph (from `mysql.role_edges`).\n- `decodeRoleEdgesTable()` (line 1025): Decodes rows from `mysql.role_edges` into `p.roleGraph` map (keyed by `toUser@toHost`, value is `roleGraphEdgesTable` listing granted roles).\n- `LoadRoleGraph()` (line 541): Loads the role graph from `mysql.role_edges`.\n- `FindRole()` (line 340): Checks if there is a direct edge between a user and a role.\n- `FindAllRole()` (line 315): BFS traversal of the role graph to find all transitively reachable roles from a set of active roles.\n- `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles()` (line 302): Filters active roles to only those actually granted to the user, then calls `FindAllRole()` for full transitive closure.\n\n### SQL Statement to Privilege Mapping\nFile: `pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go`\n- `visitInfo` struct (line 81): Represents a privilege requirement: `privilege` (mysql.PrivilegeType), `db`, `table`, `column`, `err`, `dynamicPrivs`, `dynamicWithGrant`. This is how required privileges are accumulated per statement.\n- `PlanBuilder.visitInfo []visitInfo` (line 211): Accumulated during plan building.\n- `appendVisitInfo()` in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (line 7105): Appends a static privilege requirement.\n- `appendDynamicVisitInfo()` in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (line 7096): Appends a dynamic privilege requirement.\n- `requireInsertAndSelectPriv()` in `pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go` (line 4686): Appends INSERT+SELECT for LOAD DATA / IMPORT INTO statements.\n- Throughout `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go`: Each SQL statement type (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE TABLE, DROP TABLE, ALTER TABLE, GRANT, SHOW, etc.) calls `appendVisitInfo` or `appendDynamicVisitInfo` with the specific privilege type(s) needed (e.g., `mysql.SelectPriv`, `mysql.InsertPriv`, `mysql.CreatePriv`, `mysql.ShowViewPriv`, `mysql.ExtendedPriv` for dynamic).\n- `VisitInfo4PrivCheck()` in `pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go` (line 155): Post-processes visitInfo for special cases (local temporary tables, CREATE TABLE, DROP TABLE, etc.).\n\n### Dynamic Privileges Registry\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go`\n- `dynamicPrivs` slice (line 53): Built-in dynamic privileges (BACKUP_ADMIN, RESTORE_ADMIN, SYSTEM_USER, SYSTEM_VARIABLES_ADMIN, ROLE_ADMIN, CONNECTION_ADMIN, PLACEMENT_ADMIN, DASHBOARD_CLIENT, RESTRICTED_TABLES_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_STATUS_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_VARIABLES_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_USER_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_CONNECTION_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_REPLICA_WRITER_ADMIN, RESOURCE_GROUP_ADMIN, RESOURCE_GROUP_USER).\n- `RegisterDynamicPrivilege()` (line 998): Allows plugins/extensions to register additional dynamic privileges.\n- `IsDynamicPrivilege()` (line 987): Implements `Manager.IsDynamicPrivilege()`.\n"
}

## Privilege and RBAC Enforcement in TiDB

### Key Files and Their Roles

**`pkg/privilege/privilege.go`** — Defines the `Manager` interface (the PrivilegeManager interface) with all privilege-check methods including `RequestVerification`, `RequestDynamicVerification`, `ConnectionVerification`, etc. Also provides `BindPrivilegeManager`/`GetPrivilegeManager` to attach/retrieve the manager from session context.

**`pkg/privilege/conn/conn.go`** — Defines the `AuthConn` interface used during connection-time authentication for LDAP/plugin auth.

**`pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`** — The MySQLPrivilege cache. Contains:
- `MySQLPrivilege` struct: in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables (user, db, tables_priv, columns_priv, global_priv, global_grants, role_edges, default_roles)
- `Handle` struct: thread-safe atomic-pointer wrapper for `MySQLPrivilege`
- `RequestVerification()` on `MySQLPrivilege`: core multi-level check (global → db → table → column)
- Role graph resolution: `roleGraphEdgesTable`, `decodeRoleEdgesTable()`, `FindRole()`, `FindAllRole()` (BFS), `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles()`

**`pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go`** — `UserPrivileges` implements the `Manager` interface. `RequestVerification()` here adds SEM/memory-DB guardrails before delegating to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification()`.

**`pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go`** — Defines the `visitInfo` struct mapping SQL statements to required privileges (`db`, `table`, `column`, `privilege`, `dynamicPrivs`).

**`pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go`** — During logical plan building, `appendVisitInfo()` and `appendDynamicVisitInfo()` accumulate privilege requirements for each SQL statement type (SELECT → SelectPriv, INSERT → InsertPriv, etc.).

**`pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go`** — `CheckPrivilege()` iterates visitInfo entries and calls `pm.RequestVerification()` / `pm.RequestDynamicVerification()`. `VisitInfo4PrivCheck()` handles special cases for temporary tables.

**`pkg/planner/optimize.go`** — The top-level call site: after building the logical plan, calls `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` then `CheckPrivilege` to enforce all accumulated privilege requirements.

44: // Manager is the interface for providing privilege related operations.

52: 	// RequestVerification verifies user privilege for the request.
53: 	// If table is "", only check global/db scope privileges.
54: 	// If table is not "", check global/db/table scope privileges.
55: 	// priv should be a defined constant like CreatePriv, if pass AllPrivMask to priv,

65: 
66: 	// RequestDynamicVerification verifies user privilege for a DYNAMIC privilege.

176: // RequireStr returns describe string after `REQUIRE` clause.
177: func (g *GlobalPrivValue) RequireStr() string {
178: 	require := "NONE"
179: 	switch g.SSLType {
180: 	case SslTypeAny:
181: 		require = "SSL"
182: 	case SslTypeX509:
183: 		require = "X509"
184: 	case SslTypeSpecified:
185: 		var s []string
186: 		if len(g.SSLCipher) > 0 {
187: 			s = append(s, "CIPHER")
188: 			s = append(s, "'"+g.SSLCipher+"'")
189: 		}
190: 		if len(g.X509Issuer) > 0 {
191: 			s = append(s, "ISSUER")
192: 			s = append(s, "'"+g.X509Issuer+"'")
193: 		}
194: 		if len(g.X509Subject) > 0 {
195: 			s = append(s, "SUBJECT")
196: 			s = append(s, "'"+g.X509Subject+"'")
197: 		}
198: 		if len(g.SAN) > 0 {
199: 			s = append(s, "SAN")
200: 			s = append(s, "'"+g.SAN+"'")
201: 		}
202: 		if len(s) > 0 {
203: 			require = strings.Join(s, " ")
204: 		}
205: 	}
206: 	return require
207: }

286: // MySQLPrivilege is the in-memory cache of mysql privilege tables.
287: type MySQLPrivilege struct {
288: 	// In MySQL, a user identity consists of a user + host.
289: 	// Either portion of user or host can contain wildcards,
290: 	// requiring the privileges system to use a list-like
291: 	// structure instead of a hash.
292: 
293: 	// TiDB contains a sensible behavior difference from MySQL,
294: 	// which is that usernames can not contain wildcards.
295: 	// This means that DB-records are organized in both a
296: 	// slice (p.DB) and a Map (p.DBMap).
297: 	immutable
298: 
299: 	extended
300: }

302: // FindAllUserEffectiveRoles is used to find all effective roles grant to this user.
303: // This method will filter out the roles that are not granted to the user but are still in activeRoles
304: func (p *MySQLPrivilege) FindAllUserEffectiveRoles(user, host string, activeRoles []*auth.RoleIdentity) []*auth.RoleIdentity {
305: 	grantedActiveRoles := make([]*auth.RoleIdentity, 0, len(activeRoles))
306: 	for _, role := range activeRoles {
307: 		if p.FindRole(user, host, role) {
308: 			grantedActiveRoles = append(grantedActiveRoles, role)
309: 		}
310: 	}
311: 	return p.FindAllRole(grantedActiveRoles)
312: }
313: 
314: // FindAllRole is used to find all roles grant to this user.
315: func (p *MySQLPrivilege) FindAllRole(activeRoles []*auth.RoleIdentity) []*auth.RoleIdentity {
316: 	queue, head := make([]*auth.RoleIdentity, 0, len(activeRoles)), 0
317: 	queue = append(queue, activeRoles...)
318: 	// Using breadth first search to find all roles grant to this user.
319: 	visited, ret := make(map[string]bool), make([]*auth.RoleIdentity, 0)
320: 	for head < len(queue) {
321: 		role := queue[head]
322: 		if _, ok := visited[role.String()]; !ok {
323: 			visited[role.String()] = true
324: 			ret = append(ret, role)
325: 			key := role.Username + "@" + role.Hostname
326: 			if edgeTable, ok := p.roleGraph[key]; ok {
327: 				for _, v := range edgeTable.roleList {
328: 					if _, ok := visited[v.String()]; !ok {
329: 						queue = append(queue, v)
330: 					}
331: 				}
332: 			}
333: 		}
334: 		head++
335: 	}
336: 	return ret
337: }
338: 
339: // FindRole is used to detect whether there is edges between users and roles.
340: func (p *MySQLPrivilege) FindRole(user string, host string, role *auth.RoleIdentity) bool {
341: 	rec := p.matchUser(user, host)
342: 	r := p.matchUser(role.Username, role.Hostname)
343: 	if rec != nil && r != nil {
344: 		key := rec.User + "@" + rec.Host
345: 		return p.roleGraph[key].Find(role.Username, role.Hostname)
346: 	}
347: 	return false
348: }

125: // CheckPrivilege checks the privilege for a user.
126: func CheckPrivilege(activeRoles []*auth.RoleIdentity, pm privilege.Manager, vs []visitInfo) error {
127: 	for _, v := range vs {
128: 		if v.privilege == mysql.ExtendedPriv {
129: 			hasPriv := false
130: 			for _, priv := range v.dynamicPrivs {
131: 				hasPriv = hasPriv || pm.RequestDynamicVerification(activeRoles, priv, v.dynamicWithGrant)
132: 				if hasPriv {
133: 					break
134: 				}
135: 			}
136: 			if !hasPriv {
137: 				if v.err == nil {
138: 					return plannererrors.ErrPrivilegeCheckFail.GenWithStackByArgs(v.dynamicPrivs)
139: 				}
140: 				return v.err
141: 			}
142: 		} else if !pm.RequestVerification(activeRoles, v.db, v.table, v.column, v.privilege) {
143: 			if v.err == nil {
144: 				return plannererrors.ErrPrivilegeCheckFail.GenWithStackByArgs(v.privilege.String())
145: 			}
146: 			return v.err
147: 		}
148: 	}
149: 	return nil
150: }

152: // VisitInfo4PrivCheck generates privilege check infos because privilege check of local temporary tables is different
153: // with normal tables. `CREATE` statement needs `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` privilege from the database, and subsequent
154: // statements do not need any privileges.
155: func VisitInfo4PrivCheck(ctx context.Context, is infoschema.InfoSchema, node ast.Node, vs []visitInfo) (privVisitInfo []visitInfo) {
156: 	if node == nil {
157: 		return vs
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	switch stmt := node.(type) {
161: 	case *ast.CreateTableStmt:
162: 		privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))
163: 		for _, v := range vs {
164: 			if v.privilege == mysql.CreatePriv {
165: 				if stmt.TemporaryKeyword == ast.TemporaryLocal {
166: 					// `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` privilege is required from the database, not the table.
167: 					newVisitInfo := v
168: 					newVisitInfo.privilege = mysql.CreateTMPTablePriv
169: 					newVisitInfo.table = ""
170: 					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, newVisitInfo)
171: 				} else {
172: 					// If both the normal table and temporary table already exist, we need to check the privilege.
173: 					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, v)
174: 				}
175: 			} else {
176: 				// `CREATE TABLE LIKE tmp` or `CREATE TABLE FROM SELECT tmp` in the future.
177: 				if needCheckTmpTablePriv(ctx, is, v) {
178: 					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, v)
179: 				}
180: 			}
181: 		}
182: 	case *ast.DropTableStmt:
183: 		// Dropping a local temporary table doesn't need any privileges.
184: 		if stmt.IsView {
185: 			privVisitInfo = vs
186: 		} else {
187: 			privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))
188: 			if stmt.TemporaryKeyword != ast.TemporaryLocal {
189: 				for _, v := range vs {
190: 					if needCheckTmpTablePriv(ctx, is, v) {
191: 						privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, v)
192: 					}
193: 				}
194: 			}
195: 		}
196: 	case *ast.GrantStmt, *ast.DropSequenceStmt, *ast.DropPlacementPolicyStmt:
197: 		// Some statements ignore local temporary tables, so they should check the privileges on normal tables.
198: 		privVisitInfo = vs
199: 	default:
200: 		privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))

55: // IsReadOnly check whether the ast.Node is a read only statement.
56: func IsReadOnly(node ast.Node, vars *variable.SessionVars) bool {
57: 	if execStmt, isExecStmt := node.(*ast.ExecuteStmt); isExecStmt {
58: 		prepareStmt, err := core.GetPreparedStmt(execStmt, vars)
59: 		if err != nil {
60: 			logutil.BgLogger().Warn("GetPreparedStmt failed", zap.Error(err))
61: 			return false
62: 		}
63: 		return ast.IsReadOnly(prepareStmt.PreparedAst.Stmt)
64: 	}
65: 	return ast.IsReadOnly(node)
66: }

  "text": "## Privilege and RBAC Enforcement in TiDB\n\n### PrivilegeManager Interface\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privilege.go`\nDefines the `Manager` interface with methods: `RequestVerification`, `RequestVerificationWithUser`, `RequestDynamicVerification`, `RequestDynamicVerificationWithUser`, `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege`, `ConnectionVerification`, `ShowGrants`, `ActiveRoles`, `FindEdge`, `GetDefaultRoles`, `GetAllRoles`, `DBIsVisible`, `IsDynamicPrivilege`, `MatchIdentity`, and others. Also defines `BindPrivilegeManager`/`GetPrivilegeManager` to attach/retrieve the Manager from a session context. `VerificationInfo` struct holds connection verification results.\n\nFile: `pkg/privilege/conn/conn.go`\nDefines the `AuthConn` interface used during connection authentication for LDAP/plugin auth.\n\n### MySQLPrivilege Cache\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\nThis is the central privilege cache. Key types:\n- `MySQLPrivilege` (line 287): in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables (user, db, tables_priv, columns_priv, global_priv, global_grants, role_edges, default_roles). Contains both `immutable` (raw slices) and `extended` (accelerated maps: UserMap, DBMap, TablesPrivMap, Dynamic, Global).\n- `Handle` (line 1818): thread-safe wrapper around `MySQLPrivilege` using `atomic.Pointer`. `Handle.Get()` returns the current snapshot; `Handle.Update()` reloads from KV; `ensureActiveUser()` lazily loads per-user data.\n- Record types: `UserRecord`, `dbRecord`, `tablesPrivRecord`, `columnsPrivRecord`, `globalPrivRecord`, `dynamicPrivRecord`, `defaultRoleRecord`, `roleGraphEdgesTable`.\n- SQL queries: `sqlLoadRoleGraph`, `sqlLoadUserTable`, `sqlLoadDBTable`, `sqlLoadTablePrivTable`, `sqlLoadColumnsPrivTable`, `sqlLoadGlobalPrivTable`, `sqlLoadGlobalGrantsTable`, `sqlLoadDefaultRoles`.\n- `LoadAll()` loads all tables; `LoadRoleGraph()` loads `mysql.role_edges`; individual `Load*Table()` methods load specific tables.\n\n### RequestVerification and Callers\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go`\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestVerification()` (line 142): Top-level static privilege check for current user. Handles SEM (Security Enhanced Mode) restrictions, memory/system DB rules, extension access check functions, then delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification()`.\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestVerificationWithUser()` (line 205): Same but for an explicitly specified user identity.\n- `UserPrivileges.RequestDynamicVerification()` (line 126): Checks dynamic (named string) privileges, falls back to SUPER if SEM not enabled.\n- `UserPrivileges.HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege()` (line 113): Checks dynamic priv WITHOUT SUPER fallback.\n\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\n- `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification()` (line 1333): Core multi-level check. Resolves all effective roles via `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles`, then checks global user priv → db priv → table priv → column priv in order.\n- `MySQLPrivilege.RequestDynamicVerification()` (line 1311): Checks dynamic privileges; falls back to SUPER if not restricted.\n- `MySQLPrivilege.HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege()` (line 1287): Iterates dynamic privilege records for all effective roles.\n\nCallers in `pkg/planner/`:\n- `pkg/planner/optimize.go` (line 501-505): After building the logical plan, calls `core.VisitInfo4PrivCheck()` then `core.CheckPrivilege(activeRoles, pm, visitInfo)` to enforce privileges.\n- `pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go`, `CheckPrivilege()` (line 126): Iterates `visitInfo` entries; for each static priv calls `pm.RequestVerification()`; for dynamic privs calls `pm.RequestDynamicVerification()`.\n- `pkg/planner/core/preprocess.go` (line 513): During preprocessing, calls `pm.RequestVerification()` directly for table access denial when the table is not found.\n- `pkg/planner/core/point_get_plan.go`, `checkFastPlanPrivilege()` (line 1502): Calls `pm.RequestVerification()` for point-get fast path plans.\n- `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (lines 5009, 5016): Calls `pm.RequestVerification()` for view SELECT checks and `pm.RequestVerificationWithUser()` for view definer checks.\n- `pkg/planner/core/plan_cache.go`, `checkPreparedPriv()` (line 328): Calls `core.CheckPrivilege()` for prepared statement execution.\n- `pkg/planner/core/expression_codec_fn.go` (line 91): Calls `checker.RequestVerification()` for codec function table access checks.\n\n### Role Graph Resolution\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go`\n- `roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 249): Maps role key → `*auth.RoleIdentity`; represents edges in the role graph (from `mysql.role_edges`).\n- `decodeRoleEdgesTable()` (line 1025): Decodes rows from `mysql.role_edges` into `p.roleGraph` map (keyed by `toUser@toHost`, value is `roleGraphEdgesTable` listing granted roles).\n- `LoadRoleGraph()` (line 541): Loads the role graph from `mysql.role_edges`.\n- `FindRole()` (line 340): Checks if there is a direct edge between a user and a role.\n- `FindAllRole()` (line 315): BFS traversal of the role graph to find all transitively reachable roles from a set of active roles.\n- `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles()` (line 302): Filters active roles to only those actually granted to the user, then calls `FindAllRole()` for full transitive closure.\n\n### SQL Statement to Privilege Mapping\nFile: `pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go`\n- `visitInfo` struct (line 81): Represents a privilege requirement: `privilege` (mysql.PrivilegeType), `db`, `table`, `column`, `err`, `dynamicPrivs`, `dynamicWithGrant`. This is how required privileges are accumulated per statement.\n- `PlanBuilder.visitInfo []visitInfo` (line 211): Accumulated during plan building.\n- `appendVisitInfo()` in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (line 7105): Appends a static privilege requirement.\n- `appendDynamicVisitInfo()` in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` (line 7096): Appends a dynamic privilege requirement.\n- `requireInsertAndSelectPriv()` in `pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go` (line 4686): Appends INSERT+SELECT for LOAD DATA / IMPORT INTO statements.\n- Throughout `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go`: Each SQL statement type (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE TABLE, DROP TABLE, ALTER TABLE, GRANT, SHOW, etc.) calls `appendVisitInfo` or `appendDynamicVisitInfo` with the specific privilege type(s) needed (e.g., `mysql.SelectPriv`, `mysql.InsertPriv`, `mysql.CreatePriv`, `mysql.ShowViewPriv`, `mysql.ExtendedPriv` for dynamic).\n- `VisitInfo4PrivCheck()` in `pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go` (line 155): Post-processes visitInfo for special cases (local temporary tables, CREATE TABLE, DROP TABLE, etc.).\n\n### Dynamic Privileges Registry\nFile: `pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go`\n- `dynamicPrivs` slice (line 53): Built-in dynamic privileges (BACKUP_ADMIN, RESTORE_ADMIN, SYSTEM_USER, SYSTEM_VARIABLES_ADMIN, ROLE_ADMIN, CONNECTION_ADMIN, PLACEMENT_ADMIN, DASHBOARD_CLIENT, RESTRICTED_TABLES_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_STATUS_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_VARIABLES_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_USER_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_CONNECTION_ADMIN, RESTRICTED_REPLICA_WRITER_ADMIN, RESOURCE_GROUP_ADMIN, RESOURCE_GROUP_USER).\n- `RegisterDynamicPrivilege()` (line 998): Allows plugins/extensions to register additional dynamic privileges.\n- `IsDynamicPrivilege()` (line 987): Implements `Manager.IsDynamicPrivilege()`.\n"
}
